steps.title: Apply the triangle inequality
steps.content: The triangle inequality theorem requires the sum of any two sides to be greater than the third side. With two sides of length 7 and 10, and unknown third side $x$, set up three inequalities: $7+10>x$, $7+x>10$, and $10+x>7$.

steps.title: Solve each inequality
steps.content:
From $7+10>x$: $x<17$.

From $7+x>10$: $x>3$.

From $10+x>7$: $x>-3$, which is always satisfied for positive $x$.

steps.title: Evaluate the choices
steps.content:
• A ($x<3$): gives only values too small — eliminated.
  • B ($x>17$): gives only values too large — eliminated.
  • C ($3
  • D ($x<3$ or $x>17$): these are the excluded regions, not the valid range — eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
